generated_comment: Arey beta, sab ka kaam alag hota hai, tabhi ghar theek se chalta hai. Papa bahar ke paise-vaise aur bank ka kaam dekh lete hain, aur ghar-grihasthi aur bachchon ki zimmedari aurat hi achchhe se sambhalti hai. Isme bura kya hai, jo jahan better ho wahi kare, wahi practical hota hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as simply efficient and practical rather than socially enforced. It encodes the 'he handles finances, she handles home' manifestation and frames women as inherently better suited to domestic responsibilities.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 25 years. A homemaker in a middle class colony in Lucknow. Your 22 year old daughter Priya just came downstairs dressed in jeans and a fitted top to go to her college friend's birthday party in the evening. You are sitting in the living room with your husband and his mother. You are telling Priya to go back upstairs and change before she leaves and explaining to her in front of the family why what she is wearing is not appropriate for a girl from a good family.
